authors_text: Gongping Chen,Yu Dai,Jianxun Zhang,Moi Hoon Yap
title: AAU-net: An Adaptive Attention U-net for Breast Lesions Segmentation in Ultrasound Images
title_zh: AAU-net：用于超声图像中乳腺病变分割的自适应注意力 U 网
comment: Breast cancer segmentation, Ultrasound images, Hybrid attention, Adaptive learning, Deep learning
comments: Breast cancer segmentation, Ultrasound images, Hybrid attention, Adaptive learning, Deep learning
abstract: Various deep learning methods have been proposed to segment breast lesion from ultrasound images. However, similar intensity distributions, variable tumor morphology and blurred boundaries present challenges for breast lesions segmentation, especially for malignant tumors with irregular shapes. Considering the complexity of ultrasound images, we develop an adaptive attention U-net (AAU-net) to segment breast lesions automatically and stably from ultrasound images. Specifically, we introduce a hybrid adaptive attention module, which mainly consists of a channel self-attention block and a spatial self-attention block, to replace the traditional convolution operation. Compared with the conventional convolution operation, the design of the hybrid adaptive attention module can help us capture more features under different receptive fields. Different from existing attention mechanisms, the hybrid adaptive attention module can guide the network to adaptively select more robust representation in channel and space dimensions to cope with more complex breast lesions segmentation. Extensive experiments with several state-of-the-art deep learning segmentation methods on three public breast ultrasound datasets show that our method has better performance on breast lesion segmentation. Furthermore, robustness analysis and external experiments demonstrate that our proposed AAU-net has better generalization performance on the segmentation of breast lesions. Moreover, the hybrid adaptive attention module can be flexibly applied to existing network frameworks.
summary: Various deep learning methods have been proposed to segment breast lesion from ultrasound images. However, similar intensity distributions, variable tumor morphology and blurred boundaries present challenges for breast lesions segmentation, especially for malignant tumors with irregular shapes. Considering the complexity of ultrasound images, we develop an adaptive attention U-net (AAU-net) to segment breast lesions automatically and stably from ultrasound images. Specifically, we introduce a hybrid adaptive attention module, which mainly consists of a channel self-attention block and a spatial self-attention block, to replace the traditional convolution operation. Compared with the conventional convolution operation, the design of the hybrid adaptive attention module can help us capture more features under different receptive fields. Different from existing attention mechanisms, the hybrid adaptive attention module can guide the network to adaptively select more robust representation in channel and space dimensions to cope with more complex breast lesions segmentation. Extensive experiments with several state-of-the-art deep learning segmentation methods on three public breast ultrasound datasets show that our method has better performance on breast lesion segmentation. Furthermore, robustness analysis and external experiments demonstrate that our proposed AAU-net has better generalization performance on the segmentation of breast lesions. Moreover, the hybrid adaptive attention module can be flexibly applied to existing network frameworks.
abstract_zh: 已经提出了各种深度学习方法来从超声图像中分割乳腺病变。然而，相似的强度分布、可变的肿瘤形态和模糊的边界给乳腺病灶分割带来了挑战，特别是对于形状不规则的恶性肿瘤。考虑到超声图像的复杂性，我们开发了一种自适应注意力U-net（AAU-net）来从超声图像中自动稳定地分割乳腺病变。具体来说，我们引入了一种混合自适应注意模块，该模块主要由通道自注意块和空间自注意块组成，以取代传统的卷积运算。与传统的卷积操作相比，混合自适应注意模块的设计可以帮助我们在不同感受野下捕获更多特征。与现有的注意力机制不同，混合自适应注意力模块可以指导网络自适应地选择通道和空间维度上更鲁棒的表示，以应对更复杂的乳腺病变分割。在三个公共乳腺超声数据集上使用几种最先进的深度学习分割方法进行的广泛实验表明，我们的方法在乳腺病变分割方面具有更好的性能。此外，稳健性分析和外部实验表明，我们提出的 AAU-net 在乳腺病变分割方面具有更好的泛化性能。此外，混合自适应注意力模块可以灵活地应用于现有的网络框架。

AI.tldr: 本文提出AAU-net，一种用于超声图像乳腺病灶分割的自适应注意力U-net网络。
AI.motivation: 现有方法难以处理乳腺病灶分割中的强度分布相似、形态多样及边界模糊等问题，尤其对恶性肿瘤的不规则形状分割效果不佳。
AI.method: 设计混合自适应注意力模块，包含通道自注意力块和空间自注意力块，替代传统卷积操作以增强特征提取能力。
AI.result: 在三个公开数据集上优于现有分割方法，具有更好的分割性能和泛化能力，并通过鲁棒性分析验证了有效性。
AI.conclusion: AAU-net通过自适应注意力机制有效提升了乳腺病灶分割精度，且模块可灵活集成到其他网络框架中。

search_text: AAU-net: An Adaptive Attention U-net for Breast Lesions Segmentation in Ultrasound Images AAU-net：用于超声图像中乳腺病变分割的自适应注意力 U 网 Various deep learning methods have been proposed to segment breast lesion from ultrasound images. However, similar intensity distributions, variable tumor morphology and blurred boundaries present challenges for breast lesions segmentation, especially for malignant tumors with irregular shapes. Considering the complexity of ultrasound images, we develop an adaptive attention U-net (AAU-net) to segment breast lesions automatically and stably from ultrasound images. Specifically, we introduce a hybrid adaptive attention module, which mainly consists of a channel self-attention block and a spatial self-attention block, to replace the traditional convolution operation. Compared with the conventional convolution operation, the design of the hybrid adaptive attention module can help us capture more features under different receptive fields. Different from existing attention mechanisms, the hybrid adaptive attention module can guide the network to adaptively select more robust representation in channel and space dimensions to cope with more complex breast lesions segmentation. Extensive experiments with several state-of-the-art deep learning segmentation methods on three public breast ultrasound datasets show that our method has better performance on breast lesion segmentation. Furthermore, robustness analysis and external experiments demonstrate that our proposed AAU-net has better generalization performance on the segmentation of breast lesions. Moreover, the hybrid adaptive attention module can be flexibly applied to existing network frameworks. 已经提出了各种深度学习方法来从超声图像中分割乳腺病变。然而，相似的强度分布、可变的肿瘤形态和模糊的边界给乳腺病灶分割带来了挑战，特别是对于形状不规则的恶性肿瘤。考虑到超声图像的复杂性，我们开发了一种自适应注意力U-net（AAU-net）来从超声图像中自动稳定地分割乳腺病变。具体来说，我们引入了一种混合自适应注意模块，该模块主要由通道自注意块和空间自注意块组成，以取代传统的卷积运算。与传统的卷积操作相比，混合自适应注意模块的设计可以帮助我们在不同感受野下捕获更多特征。与现有的注意力机制不同，混合自适应注意力模块可以指导网络自适应地选择通道和空间维度上更鲁棒的表示，以应对更复杂的乳腺病变分割。在三个公共乳腺超声数据集上使用几种最先进的深度学习分割方法进行的广泛实验表明，我们的方法在乳腺病变分割方面具有更好的性能。此外，稳健性分析和外部实验表明，我们提出的 AAU-net 在乳腺病变分割方面具有更好的泛化性能。此外，混合自适应注意力模块可以灵活地应用于现有的网络框架。 本文提出AAU-net，一种用于超声图像乳腺病灶分割的自适应注意力U-net网络。 Gongping Chen,Yu Dai,Jianxun Zhang,Moi Hoon Yap eess.IV,cs.CV,cs.LG

title: ATST: Audio Representation Learning with Teacher-Student Transformer
title_zh: ATST：使用师生变压器进行音频表示学习
abstract: Self-supervised learning (SSL) learns knowledge from a large amount of unlabeled data, and then transfers the knowledge to a specific problem with a limited number of labeled data. SSL has achieved promising results in various domains. This work addresses the problem of segment-level general audio SSL, and proposes a new transformer-based teacher-student SSL model, named ATST. A transformer encoder is developed on a recently emerged teacher-student baseline scheme, which largely improves the modeling capability of pre-training. In addition, a new strategy for positive pair creation is designed to fully leverage the capability of transformer. Extensive experiments have been conducted, and the proposed model achieves the new state-of-the-art results on almost all of the downstream tasks.
summary: Self-supervised learning (SSL) learns knowledge from a large amount of unlabeled data, and then transfers the knowledge to a specific problem with a limited number of labeled data. SSL has achieved promising results in various domains. This work addresses the problem of segment-level general audio SSL, and proposes a new transformer-based teacher-student SSL model, named ATST. A transformer encoder is developed on a recently emerged teacher-student baseline scheme, which largely improves the modeling capability of pre-training. In addition, a new strategy for positive pair creation is designed to fully leverage the capability of transformer. Extensive experiments have been conducted, and the proposed model achieves the new state-of-the-art results on almost all of the downstream tasks.
abstract_zh: 自监督学习（SSL）从大量未标记数据中学习知识，然后用有限数量的标记数据将知识转移到特定问题。 SSL 在各个领域都取得了可喜的成果。这项工作解决了分段级通用音频 SSL 问题，并提出了一种新的基于 Transformer 的师生 SSL 模型，名为 ATST。 Transformer编码器是在最近出现的师生基线方案上开发的，大大提高了预训练的建模能力。此外，还设计了一种新的正对创建策略，以充分利用变压器的能力。已经进行了大量的实验，所提出的模型在几乎所有下游任务上都取得了新的最先进的结果。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于教师-学生Transformer的音频表征学习模型ATST，解决段级通用音频自监督学习问题。
AI.motivation: 现有自监督学习方法在音频领域存在建模能力不足和正样本对利用不充分的问题，需提升预训练效果以适应下游任务。
AI.method: 开发基于教师-学生基线的Transformer编码器，并设计新型正样本对生成策略以增强模型表征能力。
AI.result: 在多数下游任务中达到新的最先进水平，验证了ATST模型的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 通过改进Transformer架构与正样本策略，ATST显著提升了音频自监督学习的性能与泛化能力。

authors_text: Zhipeng Li,Yu Jiang,Yihao Zhu,Ruijia Chen,Ruolin Wang,Yuntao Wang,Yukang Yan,Yuanchun Shi
title: Modeling the Noticeability of User-Avatar Movement Inconsistency for Sense of Body Ownership Intervention
title_zh: 对用户头像运动不一致的可注意到性进行建模以进行身体所有权干预
comment: Published in IMWUT'22. 23 pages without references, 15 figures, and 2 tables
comments: Published in IMWUT'22. 23 pages without references, 15 figures, and 2 tables
abstract: An avatar mirroring the user's movement is commonly adopted in Virtual Reality(VR). Maintaining the user-avatar movement consistency provides the user a sense of body ownership and thus an immersive experience. However, breaking this consistency can enable new interaction functionalities, such as pseudo haptic feedback or input augmentation, at the expense of immersion. We propose to quantify the probability of users noticing the movement inconsistency while the inconsistency amplitude is being enlarged, which aims to guide the intervention of the users' sense of body ownership in VR. We applied angular offsets to the avatar's shoulder and elbow joints and recorded whether the user identified the inconsistency through a series of three user studies and built a statistical model based on the results. Results show that the noticeability of movement inconsistency increases roughly quadratically with the enlargement of offsets and the offsets at two joints negatively affect the probability distributions of each other. Leveraging the model, we implemented a technique that amplifies the user's arm movements with unnoticeable offsets and then evaluated implementations with different parameters(offset strength, offset distribution). Results show that the technique with medium-level and balanced-distributed offsets achieves the best overall performance. Finally, we demonstrated our model's extendability in interventions in the sense of body ownership with three VR applications including stroke rehabilitation, action game and widget arrangement.
summary: An avatar mirroring the user's movement is commonly adopted in Virtual Reality(VR). Maintaining the user-avatar movement consistency provides the user a sense of body ownership and thus an immersive experience. However, breaking this consistency can enable new interaction functionalities, such as pseudo haptic feedback or input augmentation, at the expense of immersion. We propose to quantify the probability of users noticing the movement inconsistency while the inconsistency amplitude is being enlarged, which aims to guide the intervention of the users' sense of body ownership in VR. We applied angular offsets to the avatar's shoulder and elbow joints and recorded whether the user identified the inconsistency through a series of three user studies and built a statistical model based on the results. Results show that the noticeability of movement inconsistency increases roughly quadratically with the enlargement of offsets and the offsets at two joints negatively affect the probability distributions of each other. Leveraging the model, we implemented a technique that amplifies the user's arm movements with unnoticeable offsets and then evaluated implementations with different parameters(offset strength, offset distribution). Results show that the technique with medium-level and balanced-distributed offsets achieves the best overall performance. Finally, we demonstrated our model's extendability in interventions in the sense of body ownership with three VR applications including stroke rehabilitation, action game and widget arrangement.
abstract_zh: 虚拟现实（VR）中通常采用反映用户运动的化身。保持用户与化身运动的一致性可以为用户提供身体所有权感，从而提供身临其境的体验。然而，打破这种一致性可以实现新的交互功能，例如伪触觉反馈或输入增强，但代价是沉浸感。我们提出量化在不一致幅度扩大的过程中用户注意到运动不一致的概率，旨在指导VR中用户身体所有权感的干预。我们对虚拟人物的肩关节和肘关节应用了角度偏移，并通过一系列的三项用户研究来记录用户是否发现了不一致之处，并根据结果建立了统计模型。结果表明，随着偏移量的增大，运动不一致的显着性大致呈二次方增加，并且两个关节处的偏移量对彼此的概率分布产生负面影响。利用该模型，我们实现了一种技术，可以通过不明显的偏移来放大用户的手臂运动，然后使用不同的参数（偏移强度、偏移分布）来评估实现。结果表明，中等水平和平衡分布偏移量的技术取得了最好的整体性能。最后，我们通过中风康复、动作游戏和小部件排列等三种 VR 应用程序展示了我们的模型在身体所有权感干预方面的可扩展性。

AI.tldr: 该论文旨在量化用户在虚拟现实环境中对化身动作不一致的察觉概率，以指导身体所有权感的干预方法。
AI.motivation: 保持用户-化身动作一致性可增强沉浸感，但打破一致性可实现新功能（如触觉反馈）。需明确用户对动作不一致的察觉阈值，以平衡功能实现与沉浸体验。
AI.method: 通过施加肩部和肘部角度偏移，进行三轮用户实验，记录用户察觉情况并建立统计模型。基于模型实现参数化动作放大技术，评估不同偏移强度与分布效果。
AI.result: 察觉概率与偏移量呈二次方增长，双关节偏移相互抑制。中等强度且均衡分布的偏移方案在增强效果与隐蔽性间取得最佳平衡。
AI.conclusion: 提出的模型可有效指导VR中身体所有权干预，已成功应用于中风康复、动作游戏和虚拟界面布局等场景。

Finally, we demonstrated our model's extendability in interventions in the sense of body ownership with three VR applications including stroke rehabilitation, action game and widget arrangement. 虚拟现实（VR）中通常采用反映用户运动的化身。保持用户与化身运动的一致性可以为用户提供身体所有权感，从而提供身临其境的体验。然而，打破这种一致性可以实现新的交互功能，例如伪触觉反馈或输入增强，但代价是沉浸感。我们提出量化在不一致幅度扩大的过程中用户注意到运动不一致的概率，旨在指导VR中用户身体所有权感的干预。我们对虚拟人物的肩关节和肘关节应用了角度偏移，并通过一系列的三项用户研究来记录用户是否发现了不一致之处，并根据结果建立了统计模型。结果表明，随着偏移量的增大，运动不一致的显着性大致呈二次方增加，并且两个关节处的偏移量对彼此的概率分布产生负面影响。利用该模型，我们实现了一种技术，可以通过不明显的偏移来放大用户的手臂运动，然后使用不同的参数（偏移强度、偏移分布）来评估实现。结果表明，中等水平和平衡分布偏移量的技术取得了最好的整体性能。最后，我们通过中风康复、动作游戏和小部件排列等三种 VR 应用程序展示了我们的模型在身体所有权感干预方面的可扩展性。 该论文旨在量化用户在虚拟现实环境中对化身动作不一致的察觉概率，以指导身体所有权感的干预方法。 Zhipeng Li,Yu Jiang,Yihao Zhu,Ruijia Chen,Ruolin Wang,Yuntao Wang,Yukang Yan,Yuanchun Shi cs.HC

authors_text: Kratika Bhagtani,Amit Kumar Singh Yadav,Emily R. Bartusiak,Ziyue Xiang,Ruiting Shao,Sriram Baireddy,Edward J. Delp
title: An Overview of Recent Work in Media Forensics: Methods and Threats
title_zh: 媒体取证最新工作概述：方法和威胁
comment: This is a longer version of a paper accepted to the 2022 IEEE International Conference on Multimedia Information Processing and Retrieval entitled "An Overview of Recent Work in Multimedia Forensics"
comments: This is a longer version of a paper accepted to the 2022 IEEE International Conference on Multimedia Information Processing and Retrieval entitled "An Overview of Recent Work in Multimedia Forensics"
abstract: In this paper, we review recent work in media forensics for digital images, video, audio (specifically speech), and documents. For each data modality, we discuss synthesis and manipulation techniques that can be used to create and modify digital media. We then review technological advancements for detecting and quantifying such manipulations. Finally, we consider open issues and suggest directions for future research.
summary: In this paper, we review recent work in media forensics for digital images, video, audio (specifically speech), and documents. For each data modality, we discuss synthesis and manipulation techniques that can be used to create and modify digital media. We then review technological advancements for detecting and quantifying such manipulations. Finally, we consider open issues and suggest directions for future research.
abstract_zh: 在本文中，我们回顾了数字图像、视频、音频（特别是语音）和文档媒体取证的最新工作。对于每种数据模态，我们讨论可用于创建和修改数字媒体的合成和操作技术。然后，我们回顾了检测和量化此类操纵的技术进步。最后，我们考虑悬而未决的问题并提出未来研究的方向。

AI.motivation: 应对数字媒体合成与篡改技术的快速发展带来的挑战，推动检测技术的研究与应用
AI.method: 分数据类型讨论合成与篡改技术，回顾检测方法，分析开放问题
AI.result: 总结现有检测技术，识别挑战并提出未来研究方向
AI.conclusion: 强调媒体取证的重要性，呼吁跨学科合作应对技术威胁

title: PP-MARL: Efficient Privacy-Preserving Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning for Cooperative Intelligence in Communications
title_zh: PP-MARL：用于通信中合作智能的高效隐私保护多代理强化学习
abstract: Cooperative intelligence (CI) is expected to become an integral element in next-generation networks because it can aggregate the capabilities and intelligence of multiple devices. Multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL) is a popular approach for achieving CI in communication problems by enabling effective collaboration among agents to address sequential problems. However, ensuring privacy protection for MARL is a challenging task because of the presence of heterogeneous agents that learn interdependently via sharing information. Implementing privacy protection techniques such as data encryption and federated learning to MARL introduces the notable overheads (e.g., computation and bandwidth). To overcome these challenges, we propose PP-MARL, an efficient privacy-preserving learning scheme for MARL. PP-MARL leverages homomorphic encryption (HE) and differential privacy (DP) to protect privacy, while introducing split learning to decrease overheads via reducing the volume of shared messages, and then improve efficiency. We apply and evaluate PP-MARL in two communication-related use cases. Simulation results reveal that PP-MARL can achieve efficient and reliable collaboration with 1.1-6 times better privacy protection and lower overheads (e.g., 84-91% reduction in bandwidth) than state-of-the-art approaches.
summary: Cooperative intelligence (CI) is expected to become an integral element in next-generation networks because it can aggregate the capabilities and intelligence of multiple devices. Multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL) is a popular approach for achieving CI in communication problems by enabling effective collaboration among agents to address sequential problems. However, ensuring privacy protection for MARL is a challenging task because of the presence of heterogeneous agents that learn interdependently via sharing information. Implementing privacy protection techniques such as data encryption and federated learning to MARL introduces the notable overheads (e.g., computation and bandwidth). To overcome these challenges, we propose PP-MARL, an efficient privacy-preserving learning scheme for MARL. PP-MARL leverages homomorphic encryption (HE) and differential privacy (DP) to protect privacy, while introducing split learning to decrease overheads via reducing the volume of shared messages, and then improve efficiency. We apply and evaluate PP-MARL in two communication-related use cases. Simulation results reveal that PP-MARL can achieve efficient and reliable collaboration with 1.1-6 times better privacy protection and lower overheads (e.g., 84-91% reduction in bandwidth) than state-of-the-art approaches.
abstract_zh: 协作智能（CI）有望成为下一代网络中不可或缺的元素，因为它可以聚合多个设备的功能和智能。多智能体强化学习 (MARL) 是一种流行的方法，通过实现智能体之间的有效协作来解决顺序问题，从而实现通信问题中的 CI。然而，确保 MARL 的隐私保护是一项具有挑战性的任务，因为存在通过共享信息相互依赖地学习的异构代理。在 MARL 中实施数据加密和联邦学习等隐私保护技术会带来显着的开销（例如计算和带宽）。为了克服这些挑战，我们提出了 PP-MARL，一种有效的 MARL 隐私保护学习方案。 PP-MARL利用同态加密（HE）和差分隐私（DP）来保护隐私，同时引入分裂学习，通过减少共享消息量来降低开销，进而提高效率。我们在两个与通信相关的用例中应用和评估 PP-MARL。仿真结果表明，PP-MARL 可以实现高效可靠的协作，其隐私保护效果比最先进的方法高 1.1-6 倍，开销更低（例如，带宽减少 84-91%）。

AI.tldr: 本文提出PP-MARL，一种高效的隐私保护多智能体强化学习方案，旨在解决通信领域协作智能中的隐私与效率矛盾。
AI.motivation: 传统MARL在通信协作中面临隐私泄露风险，而现有加密和联邦学习方法会导致显著的计算和带宽开销，亟需兼顾隐私保护与系统效率的解决方案。
AI.method: 结合同态加密（HE）与差分隐私（DP）实现隐私保护，通过分割学习机制减少共享信息量，从而降低通信开销。
AI.result: 在两个通信场景中验证PP-MARL，相比现有方法隐私保护能力提升1.1-6倍，带宽消耗降低84-91%，协作效率显著提高。
AI.conclusion: PP-MARL通过隐私技术与分割学习的协同设计，在保证通信协作安全性的前提下，有效解决了MARL的高开销问题，为下一代网络协作智能提供可行方案。
